headline: Is Lockheed Martin’s (LMT) Golden Dome Role Quietly Redefining Its Advanced Defense Technology Narrative?
summary: In April 2026, Lockheed Martin’s Skunk Works MDCX command-and-control platform oversaw the first U.S. Navy MQ-25A Stingray flight, while the company also secured a role in the U.S. Space Force’s Golden Dome space-based interceptor effort and advanced its GPS III/IIIF satellite programs. Together with Peru’s decision to acquire 12 F-16 Block 70 aircraft and reaffirmed 2026 guidance, these developments reinforce Lockheed Martin’s position in next-generation air, space and missile defense...

headline: SpaceX To RTX:  Who's Building Trump's Golden Dome Missile Shield
summary: The U.S. Space Force has selected a dozen companies to design and demonstrate space-based interceptor prototypes for President Donald Trump‘s Golden Dome missile shield, with deliverables due by 2028. The Awards Space Systems Command (SSC) announced Friday that it has issued other transaction authority (OTA) agreements — collectively valued at up to $3.2 billion — to 12 vendors over late 2025 and early 2026.
